gradingResult.question_text: From the extract, give two ways in which the narrator shows how much she loves Bobby. You may use your own words or quotations from the text.
gradingResult.student_answer:
1 'most lovingly, with many tears' - she feels so emotional about him leaving
2 'then I wept' - one wont stop crying / tearing up.
gradingResult.llm_reasoning: The student clearly identified two distinct ways the narrator shows love for Bobby. The first point quotes "most lovingly" which directly matches mark point 4. The second point quotes "then I wept" and explains it as "one won't stop crying", which directly matches mark point 7 and also implies mark point 6 (inconsolable). Both points are well-supported by textual evidence and explanation. The maximum score is 2 marks, and the student has earned both.
gradingResult.feedback_summary: Awarded 2/2 marks. The student successfully identified two distinct ways the narrator shows love for Bobby using clear textual evidence.

mark_points_results.reasoning: Student quotes "most lovingly" directly from the text, showing the narrator's deep affection.
Student quotes "then I wept" and explains it as "one wont stop crying", showing inconsolable grief.
mark_points_results.studentCovered: 'then I wept' - one wont stop crying / tearing up.

mark_points.criteria: she sums up the totality of their love as belonging to one another
they reciprocate their love for each other
when they kiss, she cries tears of happiness
when Bobby has to leave, she is inconsolable.
